mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in retail sales signals resilient household demand and near-term revenue momentum for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the RSAFS trend is rising.
failure_condition: Reject if the 3-month change in RSAFS has Rank IC below 0.03 with 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year walk-forward window, or if positive RSAFS trends do not outperform negative trends.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above 5.0% raises equity discount rates and tightens financing conditions, reducing valuation support and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases or if average triggered returns are not below unconditional returns at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment rises while payroll growth slows, labor-market deterioration signals weakening income growth and recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp over 3 months while PAYEMS 3-month growth decelerates
failure_condition: Reject if the joint UNRATE-up and PAYEMS-deceleration trigger does not produce lower 60-day returns than either single-series signal alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in industrial production signals improving real output and operating leverage, supporting earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO trends have a 5-day forward hit rate at or below 50% and a Rank IC below 0.03 in two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A housing starts decline through its 6-month trend signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and future construction activity, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its trailing 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside crossings are followed by 60-day S&P 500 returns that are not below unconditional returns, or if the signal hit rate is below 52% out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: High policy rates combined with elevated CPI inflation indicate restrictive real financing conditions and persistent margin pressure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 3.0%
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with persistent inflation
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-FEDFUNDS and high-CPI trigger fails to outperform a model using either proxy alone, or if triggered 20-day excess returns are not negative out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment-rate crossing above its trailing 12-month low by at least 0.3pp signals a transition from tight labor conditions to rising layoff risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above its trailing 12-month low by at least 0.3pp
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE threshold crossings are not followed by below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of walk-forward cases.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth paired with rising retail sales indicates broad demand strength that offsets high financing costs and supports earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is positive and RSAFS 3-month growth is positive while FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the positive GDPC1 and RSAFS interaction under high FEDFUNDS does not produce higher 60-day returns than high FEDFUNDS alone, or if interaction alpha is negative in walk-forward tests.
